content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SAN FRANCISCO                                770 L STREET, SUITE 800
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650          SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108            TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800
                                                                    FILE NUMBER

                                    January 15, 1990
                                                                    6167.01


Ms. Kathryn Donovan
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Eighth Floor
Sacramento, California 95814

                    Re:  Request for Advice

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     The undersigned represents the California Legislative
Forum ("CLF"), a for-profit corporation formed to "foster strong
state and federal relations among members of the California
Legislature, the United States Congress and the Executive Branch
of the federal government." The CLF has a five person Board of
Directors. No elected public official is a member of the board
or an officer of the corporation. CLF is not and will not be an
Employer of a Lobbyist.

     In April 1989, CLF sponsored a dinner and reception in
Washington, D.C. for California elected and appointed public
officials, among others, in connection with the annual trip of
State Legislators to Washington, D.C. CLF was very careful to
comply with provisions of Proposition 73, including Gov't Code
§§ 85201 and 85400 and then-proposed Regulation 18540 (which was
adopted on June 5, 1989 in a much different form than that
proposed). CLF solicited and received donations from
benefactors, including many who were then Employers of a
Lobbyist. CLF advised public officials who were recipients of
dinner, beverages and the like that CLF's gifts to them would be
reportable by them as having been made by CLF, with each
benefactor being disposed and attributed the full amount of the
official's gifts. Likewise, CLF advised each benefactor to make
such a disclosure on the "activity expense" portion of its
Employer of a Lobbyist report (FPPC Form 635).

     CLF is now planning the 1990 Washington, D.C. event,
and would like to confirm that its previous approach to advising
benefactors and recipient public officials about the reporting of
gifts continues to be consistent with the law and Commission
policy. We understand that no public official "controls" the
CLF, directly or through any agent (Gov't Code § 82016), and no
"honorarium" or "speaking fee" or gift in connection therewith
will be offered by CLF to any public official.

Ms. Kathryn Donovan
January 15, 1990
Page 2


                    Please confirm the following:

          1.    The benefits to the officials, in the form of
meals, beverages, and the like, are "gifts" (Gov't Code § 82028),
even though made within six months of the elections for some of
the participating officials (2 CCR § 18228(a)).

          2.    The full value of the joint gift to each public
official made by multiple benefactors through CLF must be
attributed to each benefactor (sponsor) rather than be divided
proportionately among them for purposes of disclosure of "gifts"
and "activity expenses" by Employers of a Lobbyist (Compare In re
Smithers (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 42 and In re I.G.A. (1982) 7 FPPC
Ops. 1 with In re Art Torres (1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 31; Gov't Code
§ 86111).   That is, if the value of the gift to a public official
in attendance was $300 and was paid for by CLF from benefactor
donations solicited and pooled by CLF for such purposes, then
each donor would be attributed for disclosure purposes $300, the
full value of the gift, rather than some percentage of $300 equal
to its percentage of all donor payments to CLF.

          3.    Inasmuch as the gifts are not for "speaking fees",
they are not subject to the $2,000 per calendar year limit of
Gov't Code § 85400 (2 CCR § 18540).

          If you have any questions concerning this request for
advice, please contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        CHARLES H. BELL, JR.

CHB:ss

cc: CLF Board of Directors
